title: Test incremental load: full + incremental runs, verify no duplicates
description: Deploy changes to Fabric and run end-to-end validation: (1) full load with p_full_load=true, (2) incremental load with p_full_load=false and p_from_date set, (3) verify no duplicate records in silver_procurement and fact_procurement, (4) compare row counts and performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Full load produces correct baseline (matches previous full load row counts)
Incremental load adds only new/changed records
No duplicates in silver_procurement or fact_procurement after incremental run
Performance improvement measured (target: >50% time reduction for incremental)
incremental_load_strategy.md updated with actual performance numbers
notes: Erik runs the pipeline in Fabric UI, Claude reviews results
Check duplicate detection: SELECT COUNT(*), COUNT(DISTINCT key) FROM fact_procurement
Compare bronze_load_metadata timestamps before and after

BLOCKED 2026-07-22: gated on a physical-world prerequisite — Erik must run the pipeline in Fabric before Claude has anything to review. Empty files_affected + 'Erik runs the pipeline, Claude reviews results' means Claude cannot act first, so per FB-100 this must not sit in Pending (it made the project read as Claude-actionable when it wasn't). Additionally gated behind the task-022..026 Fabric validation batch, which changes the very MERGE grain (task-024) this test measures — running it before that batch lands would test superseded logic. Return to Pending once 022-026 are validated.

steps.instruction: Run pipeline with p_full_load=true in Fabric UI
steps.expected: Pipeline completes successfully, all tables populated

steps.instruction: Run pipeline again with p_full_load=false, p_from_date=<recent date>
steps.expected: Pipeline completes faster, only new records processed
